settings.title: Why I Became a Coach
settings.excerpt: A back injury that everyone told me to rest is the reason Ironform exists at all.
settings.body:
People sometimes ask how Ironform started, and the honest answer is that it grew out of my own bad advice.

Ten years ago I hurt my back and was told to rest and avoid lifting. Months of rest only made me weaker and more afraid to move. It was a patient coach who taught me to load properly, slowly, and the pain faded.

The lesson that stuck

What that coach gave me was not a magic exercise. It was patience and a plan. We started lighter than my pride wanted, added a little at a time, and let my body learn that movement was safe again. That slow rebuild is the thing I have tried to offer everyone since.

That experience changed how I see this work. Training is not about punishment or sweat for its own sake. It is about building a body that can do what you ask of it.

What that means on the floor

In practice, the studio runs on a few simple beliefs:

  • We start where you are, not where you think you should be.
  • Form comes before weight, every time.
  • Nobody is rushed, and no question is too basic.

If you are coming back from an injury yourself, you are exactly the person I built this place for. We will go at the pace your body sets, and we will keep notes so the progress is plain to see.

Every client who walks in here gets the kind of careful, unhurried coaching I wish I had been given. That is the whole point.
